summary: Russian forces are deploying the "Yolka" anti-drone system to kinetically intercept Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, while Ukrainian FPV units on the Zaporizhzhia front are actively targeting Russian drones, including Domaha, Darts, Chaklun, and others. Ukrainian pilots are adapting tactics by using log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas" to counter Russian jamming and extend operational range. In Iraq, Iranian-backed militias launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on the US Victory Base near Baghdad. On the ground, Russian infantry faced novel FPV threats, including a soldier being struck by two drones at once and another losing a duel after charging at a drone with a bayonet. Meanwhile, Russian officials claimed Ukraine is using "Terminator" humanoid robots, likely referring to US-made Phantom MK-1 systems, sparking concern over the rise of AI-driven warfare.
